const { extractFieldsFromExcel } = require('./utils/excelParser');
const path = require('path');

(async () => {
  const template = {
    code: 'makita_excel_statement_template',
    templateType: 'statement',
    excelCells: {
      'makita_excel_statement_template_account_no': { column: 'B', row: 4 },
      'makita_excel_statement_template_document_type': { column: 'A', row: 1 },
      'makita_excel_statement_template_statement_date': { column: 'B', row: 5 }
    }
  };
  try {
    const out = await extractFieldsFromExcel(
      path.resolve('..', '33 Statement 2026-03-02.xlsx'),
      template
    );
    const keep = {};
    for (const k of Object.keys(out)) {
      if (k === 'fullText') continue;
      keep[k] = out[k];
    }
    console.log('--- RESULT (excluding fullText) ---');
    console.log(JSON.stringify(keep, null, 2));
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('ERROR:', e.message);
    console.error(e.stack);
  }
})();
" 2>&1 | tail -120</summary>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 Now I'm ready to build out the frontend. I'll add a document type filter state that syncs with the URL search params, pass it to the API, and add a dropdown control in the toolbar alongside the existing filters. Then I'll make the column rendering and badges respond to this filter selection.

For the fallback when document type is missing, I'll either derive it from the filename or mark it as 'unknown'. The key decision is how to handle columns dynamically — I'm thinking a hybrid approach where I keep the single-row-per-document table structure but swap out which columns display based on the selected filter. When viewing "All" documents, I'll show a generic set (Type, Document No., Date, Account No., Amount, Status, Retention, Actions) that works across all types. For "Invoice" I'll show the full invoice-specific columns including Invoice To and Delivery Address. For "Credit Note" I'll use a similar layout but with Credit Note No. instead. This way the mixed view stays clean without too many columns, while individual type views show their relevant details.

Defining column configurations per filter type... I'll use a helper function to keep the row rendering clean and readable, factoring out the logic for picking which columns to display based on the document type. I need to add state for the filter, sync it with the URL and API, then conditionally render different column sets — keeping the mixed view compact with just the essentials, and tailoring the statement view to show balance aging columns instead of a single amount.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm verifying that the parsed data flows directly into the File row with all the enriched fields like `documentType` and `accountNumber`, and now I'm checking whether the `mapToStandardName` function correctly handles the specific field mappings from the test cases, particularly how it strips the template code from fields like `makita_excel_statement_template_account_no`.

The concern is that `mapToStandardName` is being called with `{ templateType: 'statement' }`, which filters fields to only those scoped to that template type — but `accountNumber` doesn't have any `templateTypes` defined, so it might get skipped by the scoping check even though it should match the alias `account_no`. Tracing through a few more examples to verify the logic: `mapToStandardName('account_no', { templateType: 'statement' })` finds the alias match in accountNumber and returns that standardName, and similarly `mapToStandardName('statement_date', { templateType: 'statement' })` and `mapToStandardName('document_type', { templateType: 'statement' })` both resolve correctly through their respective aliases. Now I'm checking edge cases in the parser changes, like whether `template.excelCells` is properly guarded against undefined values. When there's no template field mapping for documentType, the fallback to fullText for inference still works correctly. Custom fields are handled separately and won't be affected by this normalization pass. I think I've covered all the edge cases — let me mark this verification complete.</details></think>

_**Agent (premium)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm seeing that the test-parse endpoint doesn't include the `code` field when building the template object, so `template.code` ends up undefined and the prefix becomes empty. This means the Excel cell keys get constructed as just `${testTemplateCode}_${cell.fieldName}` instead of including the actual code value.

When I trace through `mapToStandardName` with a prefixed field name like `makita_excel_statement_template_account_no`, it doesn't find a direct match in STANDARD_FIELDS, but the function walks backward through the last 1-3 parts and successfully matches `account_no` to `accountNumber`, so the routing still works. Though I'm realizing the test-parse endpoint probably should be passing `code` along with the other template properties.

The real question is whether the test-parse output will now include the canonical fields like `documentType`, `accountNumber`, and `statementDate` alongside the prefixed versions, which would confirm the fix is working. I should also verify that the ExcelTemplateBuilder Test Results panel can handle displaying these new canonical fields without breaking.</details></think>

_**Agent (premium)**_

All done. Here's the summary.

## Files changed

- **`backend/utils/excelParser.js`** — root cause. After the main extraction loop, the parser now mirrors every prefixed field under its canonical standard name (`makita_excel_statement_template_account_no` → also stored as `accountNumber`) and builds a `fieldLabels` map, matching what the PDF flow already does. The `documentType` block previously short-circuited on any `*_document_type*` key (including the prefixed one) and never wrote the canonical lowercase value — replaced with proper normalization that runs from the extracted cell value, falling back to `fullText` inference.
- **`backend/routes/unallocated.js`** — accepts `?documentType=invoice|credit_note|statement|unknown` and filters via the same `parsedData::text` ILIKE pattern already used for `accountNumber`/`invoiceNumber`. `unknown` matches rows whose `parsedData` has no `documentType` key at all (older parses).
- **`frontend/src/pages/Unallocated.js`** — added a Document Type dropdown in the toolbar (next to "All Reasons"), wired into URL state, the API call, and `Reset`. The fallback for the row badge is now `unknown` (with a muted style and "Unknown" label) instead of silently calling everything an Invoice. The header set, skeleton row, empty-state colSpan, and row cells now branch on the active filter:
  - **All / Invoice / Credit Note** — current invoice-style columns; just renames `Invoice No.` → `Credit Note No.` for the credit-note view.
  - **Statement** — `Statement Date | Account No. | Total Balance | Current | 1-30 | 31-60 | 61-90 | 91+`.
  - **Unknown** — minimal generic set: `Document No. | Date | Account No. | Amount`.

## Why this fixes the three symptoms

1. **"They show as Invoices"** — the JSON you pasted had `makita_excel_statement_template_document_type: "STATEMENT"` but no canonical `documentType` field; the row renderer's `|| 'invoice'` fallback kicked in. The parser now writes `documentType: 'statement'` (and `accountNumber: '33'`, `statementDate: '2026-03-02'`, plus all aging buckets and `fieldLabels`), so the badge and cells will populate correctly the next time a statement is parsed.
2. **"No way to filter them"** — new dropdown with All / Invoices / Credit Notes / Statements / Unknown, persisted in the URL.
3. **"Table columns are wrong"** — the column set switches when you pick **Statements**, replacing `Invoice To / Delivery Address / PO Number / Amount` with the aging buckets and total balance.

## Commands to run

```bash
# Frontend (no new deps; just rebuild)
cd frontend && npm run build

# Backend (no new deps; restart the worker + API so the parser change loads)
cd backend && pm2 restart all   # or however you restart on your server
```

No DB migration needed — this is a logic fix, not a schema change.

## What to test manually

1. **Re-import the 33 statement xlsx** through `/api/statements/import` (or the FTP path). The Test Parse panel should now show `documentType: "statement"`, `accountNumber: "33"`, `statementDate: "2026-03-02"`, and a `fieldLabels` map alongside the prefixed fields.
2. If the import still fails to allocate (e.g., no CORP company `33` exists), open the Unallocated page:
   - The badge should now say **Statement**, not Invoice.
   - The Document Type dropdown should let you filter to **Statements**.
   - With Statements selected, the columns become Statement Date / Account No. / Total Balance / aging buckets.
3. Existing invoice and credit-note unallocated rows should still render correctly under **All Types** (default) — no change in behaviour for those.
4. Pick **Unknown** to find any old pre-fix rows that came in without a documentType written into `parsedData`.

One thing worth being aware of: the backend filter looks at `parsedData::text` for a `"documentType":"statement"` substring, so older rows imported before this fix won't match the **Statements** filter — they'll show under **Unknown**. That's expected. If you want me to write a one-off backfill script that re-classifies old rows by walking their prefixed fields, say the word.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 Looking at the frontend constraints, `deliveryAddress` is locked to invoices and credit notes, so I need to create a separate `customerAddress` field specifically for statements. I'll add this new field to both the standard fields configuration and the backend schema to make it available in the PDF template builder.

For the column display issue, the user likely tried filtering to Statements but the view didn't update properly, or they're viewing the "All Types" mixed mode where invoice columns are being applied to statement rows. The fix is to use a neutral column set when "All Types" is selected — one that works across all document types so statement rows display their relevant fields (Date, Account, Amount) instead of showing dashes for invoice-specific columns like "Invoice To" or "Delivery Address." I need to finalize the approach: add `customerAddress` as a standard field for statements, switch "All Types" to neutral columns so statement rows don't render with empty invoice-specific fields, and make sure the frontend rebuild picks up these changes (either through HMR on dev or a fresh build in production).</details></think>

The real issue is that "All Types" shouldn't force invoice-shaped columns with all dashes for statements. _**Agent (premium)**_

Pushed as `ac73d09`. Quick recap of what's now live and why your row looked the way it did:

## Why the row in your HTML looked like an invoice

Your pasted row had 12 cells with `Invoice To / Delivery Address / PO Number` columns — that's the **invoice/credit_note** column set. There are only two ways to land on those columns:

1. The dropdown is on **All Types** (the default). My previous commit kept the invoice-style layout for "All Types" too, which meant a Statement row in mixed view rendered with three empty `-` cells for invoice-only fields. That's the bug you spotted.
2. The dropdown is on **Statements** but the frontend bundle didn't reload. (HMR usually picks it up; for a production build you need `npm run build` and a hard refresh.)

I've now fixed (1) properly — see below. If you also see invoice columns when **Statements is explicitly selected**, that's case (2): force-refresh (`Ctrl+Shift+R`) or rebuild the frontend.

## Files changed (this commit)

- **`backend/utils/standardFields.js`** + **`frontend/src/utils/standardFields.js`** — added a new statement-only standard field:

```javascript
customerAddress: {
  standardName: 'customerAddress',
  displayName: 'Customer Address',
  description: 'Customer / account billing address printed on the statement header',
  isCrucial: false,
  isMandatory: false,
  parsingOrder: 11,
  templateTypes: ['statement'],
  aliases: ['customer_address', 'customeraddress', 'account_address', 'billing_address', 'address']
}
```

  The PDF Coordinate Template Builder pulls fields via `getAvailableFields(templateType)` (`frontend/src/components/TemplateBuilder.js:33`), which already filters on `templateTypes`, so **Customer Address** will automatically show up as a mappable field next time you open a Statement PDF template — no template-builder changes needed.

- **`frontend/src/pages/Unallocated.js`** — column behavior:

| Filter | Columns |
|---|---|
| **All Types** *(new neutral set)* | Type, Document No., Date, Account No., Amount |
| **Invoices** / **Credit Notes** | Doc No., Date/Tax Point, Account No., Invoice To, Delivery Address, PO Number, Amount |
| **Statements** | Statement Date, Account No., Total Balance, Current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 91+ |
| **Unknown** | Type, Document No., Date, Account No., Amount |

  In the neutral view, statement rows render `-` for Document No. (statements don't carry one) and use **totalBalance** for the Amount column instead of `totalAmount`, so they show meaningful figures.

## Commands to run

```bash
# Frontend (production build)
cd frontend && npm run build

# Backend (parser + standardFields change)
cd backend && pm2 restart all   # or your usual restart
```

## What to test

1. **Statement PDF template builder** — open a statement PDF template; "Customer Address" should appear in the field dropdown. Map it to a coordinate; on test parse the value should appear under `customerAddress` (and a `customerAddress` entry in `fieldLabels`).
2. **Unallocated page, default view** — your sample row should now show: `Type=Statement, Document No.=-, Date=02 Mar 2026, Account No.=33, Amount=£<totalBalance>` (no awkward Invoice To / PO empties).
3. **Unallocated page, Statements filter** — selecting Statements switches to the statement-specific column set. If it doesn't switch on first try, hard-refresh the browser to bypass any cached bundle.

_**Agent (premium)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I've got three things to fix here. First, I need to strip out all those debug console logs that are cluttering the output. Second, there's an issue with the filter logic that's always returning "No unallocated documents found" even when there should be results. And third, I need to update the table header to display "INV / CRN / STMT No." instead of "Document No." Let me start by removing those noisy logs from the PDF code.

For the filter problem, I'm looking at how it's currently checking for documentType. The existing accountNumber filter works by casting the parsedData JSONB column to text and using an iLike pattern match. But there's a potential issue with how Postgres formats JSONB when cast to text — it produces a canonical form with specific key ordering and no extra whitespace, so the pattern might not be matching correctly. The filter is looking for `"documentType":"statement"` but depending on how the data is stored or formatted, it might not find matches in older database rows that don't have this field, or the JSON structure might be different than expected.

I'm thinking the real issue is that legacy data imported before the parser fix was deployed probably doesn't have the canonical documentType field at all — only the prefixed fields like `makita_excel_statement_template_*`. So when the user filters for "Statements," it finds nothing because those old rows lack the documentType key entirely. A simpler fix than backfilling would be to make the filter more flexible by matching against the prefixed field names directly, which would work for both old and new data without requiring a migration.

Updating the filter logic... Building the filter query...

The core issue is that older imported documents don't have the canonical `documentType` field yet, so I need to check multiple places: the new canonical field, the legacy prefixed keys, and the fullText as a fallback. The frontend badge shows "Statement" because it's checking those same fallback fields, but the backend filter isn't matching them. If users haven't re-imported their files since the parser fix was deployed, their database rows still have the old structure, which is why the filter returns nothing. Still writing filter logic... Hmm, so if the badge shows "Statement", then `parsed.documentType` must actually be set to 'statement' in the database. That means the canonical field is there, so the filter pattern should work. Let me trace through the iLike logic again — the pattern `%"documentType":"statement"%` should match any JSON text containing that exact substring. Unless there's something about how Postgres handles the case sensitivity or the JSON formatting that I'm missing here.

Let me think about what could be going wrong. Either the frontend isn't sending the parameter, the backend isn't receiving it, or the filter isn't actually being applied to the right column. I should also check whether the `parsedData` column is stored as actual JSONB or just as a text string — if it's text, the formatting might be different than I'm expecting.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm tracking down the noisy logs and suspect the filter issue stems from how the JSONB data is being cast and compared. The `notILike` pattern for unknown values might be conflicting with the broader `iLike` filter in the where clause, so I need to examine that logic more carefully.

Looking at the actual query composition: when a user selects "Statements", the route should match files where the parsed data contains `"documentType":"statement"`, but this gets combined with status filters like `unallocated`. The row the user showed has both properties, so either the status filter is excluding it, the documentType filter isn't matching correctly, or there's a bug in how these conditions are ANDed together. I'm also wondering if `parsedData` is storing `documentType` in a different format than expected, or if the `failureReason` filter is interfering when set to its default value.

Let me trace through the logic more carefully: when `failureReason` is set to a specific value like `'unallocated'`, it adds an OR condition checking both that field and null values, then the documentType filter adds another AND condition on top. This should still work correctly. Rather than keep speculating about column names or aliases, I should just test the route with a more permissive fallback that checks multiple possible locations for the documentType value.

But looking at the user's actual complaint — they're saying the filter always returns nothing regardless of what's selected — that suggests the problem might be broader than just documentType. If selecting any filter breaks the query, maybe the issue is with how `documentTypeFilter` is being passed in, or there's a problem with the Op import itself at the top of the route.</details></think>

_**Agent (premium)**_

_**Agent (premium)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm looking at the filter behavior—the user is seeing "No unallocated documents found" even when they select "Statements" from the dropdown and the query parameter gets set correctly. The issue seems to be that the filter is being applied but not returning the expected results, so I need to trace through how the documents are being filtered when that specific document type is selected. text adds spaces after colons and commas in the canonical JSON form. So my iLike pattern `%"documentType":"statement"%` without a space won't match the actual stored format `"documentType": "statement"` with a space — that's likely the bug. I need to verify whether the existing `accountNumber` filter actually works with the same pattern, or if there's something else going on with how Postgres handles the JSONB to text conversion. I'm overthinking the space issue — let me just use Postgres's JSON operators instead. The `->>` operator extracts JSONB values as text directly, which should sidestep the casting problem entirely. I can use that with a case-insensitive comparison to cleanly query the documentType field without worrying about formatting inconsistencies. Still writing the filter logic... I think the real issue is the JSONB format — I need to use the `->>` operator to extract text values properly instead of casting the whole column. I'll make the filter more robust by checking the canonical documentType field, and also fall back to matching legacy data that might have prefixed document type fields or hints in the fullText.

Writing the filter logic... Finalizing the query logic...

I'm settling on using `sequelize.where` with the LOWER function to match the canonical documentType field from the JSONB data, handling both the 'unknown' case where the field is null and the standard document types. I should also clean up the console logs and update the column header in the All Types view from "Document No." to "INV / CRN / STMT No." to better reflect what's being displayed.</details></think>

_**Agent (premium)**_

OK three things — let me check why the filter is broken, then fix everything.

**Diagnosis of the filter bug:** The route uses `iLike` against the JSONB column cast to text. Postgres' `JSONB::text` formatting can produce `"documentType": "statement"` *with* a space after the colon depending on version, but my iLike pattern is `%"documentType":"statement"%` (no space). When that mismatches, you get zero rows even though the data is right. The fix is to use the JSONB `->>` operator instead, which extracts the value as plain text regardless of formatting.

_**Agent (premium)**_

_**Agent (premium)**_

Pushed as `df7856c`. Summary:

## Files changed

- **`backend/routes/unallocated.js`** — replaced the brittle `cast(parsedData, text) ILIKE '%"documentType":"statement"%'` with the JSONB `->>` operator:
  ```js
  sequelize.where(
    sequelize.fn('LOWER', sequelize.literal(`"File"."parsedData"->>'documentType'`)),
    trimmed
  )
  ```
  This is why the filter was always returning empty: Postgres' `jsonb::text` inserts a space after the colon on most modern versions, so `"documentType":"statement"` (the literal pattern) never matched the actual stored text `"documentType": "statement"` (with space). `->>` doesn't care about formatting — it extracts the JSON field as plain text.
- **`frontend/src/pages/Unallocated.js`** — neutral "All Types" view column header is now **INV / CRN / STMT No.** instead of the generic "Document No.".
- **`frontend/src/components/TemplateBuilder.js`** — removed all the noisy console logs:
  - `Mouse down: Object`
  - `🖱️ Mouse move: Object`
  - `========== NORMALIZED COORDINATE CONVERSION ==========` (full block)
  - `Sending request to backend:` (full block)
  - `========== EXTRACTION RESULT ==========` (full block)
  - `Saving PDF template with coordinates: Object`
  - `Render cancelled (this is normal when changing zoom/page)`

  All useful diagnostics are still produced server-side and via toasts; nothing user-facing is lost.

## Commands to run

```bash
# Backend (filter fix)
cd backend && pm2 restart all

# Frontend (header label + log strip)
cd frontend && npm run build
```

## What to test

1. **PDF Template Builder** — open a Statement PDF template, draw a region. Console should be clean of the mouse/coordinate spam. Toasts still show extraction success/failure.
2. **Unallocated page, Statements filter** — should now actually return your statement rows. (If it still shows empty after restarting the backend, it means the row in the DB doesn't have a canonical `documentType` written yet — i.e. it was imported before the parser fix. Pick **Unknown** to find those, or re-import the file to refresh `parsedData`.)
3. **Neutral view** — header now reads "INV / CRN / STMT No." in the "All Types" view.

---

_**User**_

For the menu can change the dashboard icon to

IconHomeFilled
Statements to IconFileAnalyticsFilled
Invoice to IconFileInvoiceFilled

---

_**Agent (premium)**_

Pushed as `bf31898`.

## Files changed

`frontend/src/components/Layout/Layout.js` — two sidebar icons swapped to the official Tabler filled variants:

| Item | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | outline home with chimney (`icon icon-1`, stroke-based) | `IconHomeFilled` — `icon-tabler icons-tabler-filled icon-tabler-home` |
| **Statements** | outline file with three lines | `IconFileAnalyticsFilled` — `icon-tabler icons-tabler-filled icon-tabler-file-analytics` |
| **Invoices** | (unchanged) | already on `IconFileInvoiceFilled` (`icon-tabler-file-invoice` + matching path data) |

Path data taken verbatim from Tabler's official sources, then expanded into the verbose space-separated form the rest of this file uses, so the diff style is consistent.

## To deploy

```bash
cd frontend && npm run build
```

Hard-refresh the browser after deploy if the old icons are cached.

_**Agent (premium)**_

Pushed. The fix is in `frontend/src/pages/UnallocatedView.js`.

### What changed

**`frontend/src/pages/UnallocatedView.js`**
- `fetchDocument` now also maps the canonical statement fields off `parsedData` into `editingData`: `statementDate`, `customerAddress`, `totalBalance`, `currentAmount`, `overdue1To30`, `overdue31To60`, `overdue61To90`, `overdue91Plus`.
- The invoice-shaped fields (Date/Tax Point, Invoice Number, PO Number, Total, VAT Amount, Delivery Address, Goods Amount, Invoice To) are now wrapped in a guard that skips them when `documentType === 'statement'`.
- New statement-only block underneath. Top-level inputs: `Statement Date`, `Customer Address`, `Total Balance`. The five aging buckets (`Current`, `Overdue 1-30`, `Overdue 31-60`, `Overdue 61-90`, `Overdue 91+`) render in a responsive 2-column grid so the form stays compact.

Document Type and Account Number / Customer Number stay on screen for both shapes (statements still need the account number to allocate). Save Changes / Attempt Allocation / Cancel are unchanged and post the merged `editingData` — so when the parser eventually emits the statement fields, saving will persist them correctly.

### What to test manually
1. Open an unallocated **statement** (e.g. the one for account `33`) → Extracted tab should now show: Document Type, Account Number, Statement Date, Customer Address, Total Balance, and the five aging-bucket inputs. No PO/VAT/Delivery/Invoice To.
2. Open an unallocated **invoice** or **credit note** → original invoice fields render exactly as before.
3. Edit the account number on a statement and click **Attempt Allocation** → still routes through the existing allocation path.
4. The **JSON** tab still shows the full raw `parsedData` for both types.

```
commit df7856c3f2fefb65745168f98f012f167505c17d
Author: Rob &lt;rob@nybble.co.uk&gt;
Date:   Fri May 22 21:28:41 2026 +0100

    Unallocated: fix documentType filter; relabel neutral header; quiet PDF builder logs
    
    - unallocated route: switch documentType filter from cast(parsedData,text) +
      ILIKE to JSONB -&gt;&gt; operator. The text-cast approach was brittle because
      Postgres' jsonb::text inserts a space after the colon on some versions,
      silently breaking literal substring matches like %"documentType":"statement"%
      and producing 'No documents found' for rows the data clearly contained.
      -&gt;&gt; extracts the value cleanly regardless of formatting.
    - Unallocated page: rename neutral 'Document No.' column header to
      'INV / CRN / STMT No.' so mixed-type rows have a clearer column meaning.
    - TemplateBuilder: remove noisy mouse-move / coordinate-conversion / extraction
      debug logs that were leaking to the production console.

diff --git a/frontend/src/components/TemplateBuilder.js b/frontend/src/components/TemplateBuilder.js
index a18339b..69e0c7f 100644
--- a/frontend/src/components/TemplateBuilder.js
+++ b/frontend/src/components/TemplateBuilder.js
@@ -211,7 +211,7 @@ const TemplateBuilder = forwardRef(({ template, onSave, onCancel }, ref) =&gt; {
     } catch (error) {
       // Ignore cancellation errors
       if (error.name === 'RenderingCancelledException' || error.message?.includes('cancelled')) {
-        console.log('Render cancelled (this is normal when changing zoom/page)');
+        // Expected when zoom/page changes mid-render; silently ignore.
         return;
       }
       console.error('Error rendering PDF:', error);
@@ -339,8 +339,6 @@ const TemplateBuilder = forwardRef(({ template, onSave, onCancel }, ref) =&gt; {
     const clampedX = Math.max(0, Math.min(x, canvasRect.width));
     const clampedY = Math.max(0, Math.min(y, canvasRect.height));
     
-    console.log('Mouse down:', { x, y, clampedX, clampedY, canvasRect });
-    
     setStartPos({ x: clampedX, y: clampedY });
```

```bash
cd "/media/rob/SEAGATE_4TB/Makita - Invoice Portal 2026" && git add frontend/src/components/TemplateBuilder.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
TemplateBuilder: restore pdfX/pdfY conversion (broken by log cleanup)

The previous commit (df7856c) removed the pdfX/pdfY/pdfWidth_coord/
pdfHeight_coord declarations along with their debug console.logs, but
those values are still consumed by coordinatesData and regionData as a
legacy fallback (the renderer reads region.x/y/width/height when

)" && git push
```

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (premium)**_

Fixed and pushed (`0ef4334`).

### What happened
In the earlier log-cleanup commit (`df7856c`), I removed `pdfX/pdfY/pdfWidth_coord/pdfHeight_coord` along with the noisy `console.log` block they fed. But those four values are still referenced further down in `coordinatesData` and `regionData` because the renderer (around lines 1119–1144) falls back to `region.x/y/width/height` (PDF-point coords) when `region.normalized` is missing on older saved templates. ESLint's `no-undef` caught it.

### What I restored
Just the conversion math (no logs), placed right after `normalizedCoords`:

```javascript
const pdfPageSize = pdfPage.view;
const pdfPageWidth = pdfPageSize[2] - pdfPageSize[0];
const pdfPageHeight = pdfPageSize[3] - pdfPageSize[1];
const pdfX = normalizedCoords.left * pdfPageWidth;
const pdfWidth_coord = (normalizedCoords.right - normalizedCoords.left) * pdfPageWidth;
const pdfHeight_coord = (normalizedCoords.bottom - normalizedCoords.top) * pdfPageHeight;
const pdfY = pdfPageHeight - (normalizedCoords.bottom * pdfPageHeight);
```

### Run again
```bash
cd frontend && npm run build
```
Build should compile cleanly now.

_**Agent (premium)**_

Implemented — Statements now has an in-app Excel preview using SheetJS.

Based on the SheetJS workbook preview approach (`XLSX.read` + `XLSX.utils.sheet_to_html`) from the docs: [SheetJS CE Docs](https://docs.sheetjs.com/docs/).

**What changed**
- Added `SheetJS` usage in `frontend/src/pages/Statements.js`.
- Added a new **Preview XLS** action button for statement rows that have an XLS/XLSX file.
- Added an **XLS Preview modal**:
  - Fetches the XLS file through your authenticated endpoint (`/api/statements/:id/download-xls`)
  - Parses workbook in browser with `xlsx`
  - Renders sheet tabs (if multiple worksheets)
  - Displays selected sheet as read-only HTML table preview

**Files changed**
- `frontend/src/pages/Statements.js` — added XLS preview state, fetch/parse logic, preview button, and modal UI.

**Commands to run**
- `cd frontend && npm install`
- `npm run build`

(`npm run build` failed in this environment because `react-scripts` is not installed locally there, so dependencies need installing first.)

**What to test manually**
1. Go to `Statements`.
2. Find a row with XLS available.
3. Click **Preview XLS**.
4. Confirm modal opens and displays worksheet content.
5. If workbook has multiple sheets, switch tabs and confirm each renders.
6. Confirm existing **View (PDF)** and **Download XLS/PDF** actions still work.

_**Agent (premium)**_

Yes — fixed so Excel docs are treated as **XLS Preview**, not PDF.

### Files changed
- `frontend/src/pages/UnallocatedView.js`  
  - Added file-type aware preview mode (`pdf` vs `xls`).
  - Header now shows `XLS Preview` for `.xls/.xlsx` files.
  - Added real XLS rendering in the left preview panel using SheetJS (`xlsx`) with sheet tabs.
  - Kept existing PDF canvas preview flow for PDF files.
- `backend/routes/unallocated.js`  
  - Added `GET /api/unallocated/:id/view-file` to serve original unallocated file inline with correct MIME type (`pdf`, `xls`, `xlsx`), used by the XLS preview fetch.

### Commands to run
- `cd backend && npm run start` (or your normal backend restart command)
- `cd frontend && npm run build`

### What to test manually
1. Open an unallocated **Excel** document view:
   - Left card title should be `XLS Preview`.
   - Worksheet data should render.
   - If workbook has multiple sheets, sheet tab buttons should switch content.
2. Open an unallocated **PDF** document view:
   - Left card title should be `PDF Preview`.
   - Existing PDF preview should still render as before.
3. Confirm extracted data tab behavior is unchanged for save/attempt-allocation.

_**Agent (premium)**_

Done — the Document Type input now displays capitalized text (`Statement` instead of `statement`) while keeping the underlying stored value unchanged.

### Files changed
- `frontend/src/pages/UnallocatedView.js` — added `style={{ textTransform: 'capitalize' }}` to Document Type input.
- `frontend/src/pages/Unallocated.js` — same change in the modal/list edit view for consistency.

### Commands to run
- `cd frontend && npm run build`

### What to test manually
1. Open an unallocated statement in View/Edit.
2. Confirm Document Type shows `Statement` in the input.
3. Save/attempt allocation and verify filtering still works (since stored value stays lowercase).

```
diff --git a/backend/routes/unallocated.js b/backend/routes/unallocated.js
index a456341..70d869b 100644
--- a/backend/routes/unallocated.js
+++ b/backend/routes/unallocated.js
@@ -403,6 +403,59 @@ router.get('/:id/view-pdf', async (req, res) =&gt; {
   }
 });
 
+// View unallocated original file inline (PDF/XLS/XLSX)
+router.get('/:id/view-file', async (req, res) =&gt; {
+  try {
+    if (req.user.role === 'external_user') {
+      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Access denied' });
+    }
+
+    const file = await File.findByPk(req.params.id);
+
+    if (!file) {
+      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Document not found' });
+    }
+
+    if (file.status !== 'unallocated' &amp;&amp; file.status !== 'failed' &amp;&amp; file.status !== 'duplicate') {
+      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Document is not unallocated, failed, or duplicate' });
+    }
+
+    if (!file.filePath) {
+      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'No document file available' });
+    }
+
+    let filePath;
+    if (path.isAbsolute(file.filePath)) {
+      filePath = file.filePath;
+    } else {
+      filePath = path.join(__dirname, '..', file.filePath);
+      if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
+        filePath = file.filePath;
+      }
+    }
+
+    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
+      console.error(&#96;File not found: ${filePath} (original: ${file.filePath})&#96;);
+      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Document file not found on server' });
+    }
+
+    const ext = path.extname(file.fileName || filePath).toLowerCase();
+    const mimeByExt = {
+      '.pdf': 'application/pdf',
+      '.xls': 'application/vnd.ms-excel',
+      '.xlsx': 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'
+    };
+    const contentType = mimeByExt[ext] || 'application/octet-stream';
+
+    res.setHeader('Content-Type', contentType);
+    res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', &#96;inline; filename="${file.fileName}"&#96;);
+    res.sendFile(filePath);
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error('Error viewing unallocated document file:', error);
+    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
+  }
+});
+
 // Update parsed data and requeue for processing - GA + Admin + Manager only
 router.put('/:id', requirePermission('UNALLOCATED_EDIT'), async (req, res) =&gt; {
   try {

+    try {

+      const response = await fetch(&#96;${API_BASE_URL}/api/statements/${statement.id}/download-xls&#96;, {

+        throw new Error(errorData.message || 'Failed to fetch XLS file');

+

+      const workbook = XLSX.read(fileBuffer, { type: 'array' });

+      setXlsPreviewSheets(parsedSheets);
```

```
46164c5 Unallocated: rename neutral column header to 'INV / CRN No.'
0ef4334 TemplateBuilder: restore pdfX/pdfY conversion (broken by log cleanup)
```

_**User**_

Is there anything in the SheetsJS documentation that makes it look nicer? Make it look like an Excel sheet?

---

_**Agent (premium)**_

Yes — but with an important caveat:

- **SheetJS CE** can generate HTML tables (`sheet_to_html`) quickly, but it does **not** fully replicate native Excel styling/behavior by itself.
- For richer workbook styling fidelity, SheetJS docs point to **SheetJS Pro** features (advanced styling/template handling).

From the docs: [SheetJS CE Overview](https://docs.sheetjs.com/docs/).

### What you can do in CE (and we can apply now)
- Wrap preview table in an “Excel-like” container.
- Add CSS for:
  - Gridlines (`border-collapse`, thin borders)
  - Header row shading / sticky row
  - Calibri-like font
  - Cell padding and row height
  - Zebra rows + hover
  - Frozen top row effect via sticky `thead`
- Use `sheet_to_html` options (like `id`) so we can target table CSS cleanly.

### What CE won’t do perfectly
- Full native workbook styling parity (merged styles, advanced formatting fidelity, formulas rendering exactly like Excel UI, etc.).

If you want, I can implement an **Excel-style preview theme** right now (light grid, sticky headers, column alignment, cleaner spacing) so it visually feels much closer to Excel in your modal.

---

_**User**_

Well its just a table anyway? Can we make it look like an Excel sheet? Some nice styling?

---

_**Agent (premium)**_

Yep — we can style it to feel much closer to Excel. I’ll add an Excel-style theme (gridlines, header shading, sticky header row, Calibri-like font, zebra/hover rows) to the XLS preview in both Statements and Unallocated view.

_**Agent (premium)**_

Implemented — Statements now follows the same pattern as Invoices/Credit Notes for actions, permissions, and page flow.

### What changed

- **Actions now align to `View / Download / Edit / Delete`**
  - In `Statements` list, action buttons are now:
    - `View` (always, opens dedicated view page)
    - `Download` (single generic download button)
    - `Edit` (opens dedicated edit page)
    - `Delete` (existing delete flow)
- **Status badges switched to `-lt` variants**
  - `draft -> bg-secondary-lt`
  - `sent -> bg-info-lt`
  - `acknowledged -> bg-success-lt`
  - `disputed -> bg-warning-lt`
- **No modal flow for View/Edit anymore**
  - Added dedicated pages:
    - `statements/:id/view`
    - `statements/:id/edit`
- **View/Download tracking stays correct**
  - `View` uses statement view endpoints (marks viewed)
  - `Download` uses download endpoint (marks downloaded)
  - Added backend XLS preview endpoint for proper **view** tracking of Excel previews

---

### Files changed

- `frontend/src/pages/Statements.js`  
  Switched table actions to route-based view/edit and generic download; status badges now `-lt`.
- `frontend/src/pages/StatementView.js` *(new)*  
  Dedicated statement view page with file-type preview (PDF/XLS), XLS sheet tabs, and download buttons.
- `frontend/src/pages/StatementEdit.js` *(new)*  
  Dedicated statement edit page (moved modal form behavior into standalone route page).
- `frontend/src/App.js`  
  Added routes for `statements/:id/view` and `statements/:id/edit`.
- `backend/routes/statements.js`  
  Added `GET /api/statements/:id/view-xls` for inline XLS preview with **view** tracking.
- `backend/utils/permissions.js`  
  Updated `STATEMENTS_*` permissions to align with Invoice/Credit Note role model.
- `frontend/src/context/PermissionContext.js`  
  Same `STATEMENTS_*` permission alignment on frontend.

---

### Commands to run

- `cd backend && npm run start` (or your normal backend restart command)
- `cd frontend && npm run build`

---

### What to test manually

1. **Statements list row actions**
   - Confirm buttons are exactly `View / Download / Edit / Delete` (permission-dependent).
2. **Status badge style**
   - Confirm statement status badges use light variants (`bg-*-lt`).
3. **View page route**
   - Click `View` from list, ensure it opens `/statements/:id/view`.
   - Test both PDF and XLS previews.
4. **Edit page route**
   - Click `Edit`, ensure it opens `/statements/:id/edit`.
   - Save requires edit reason and persists updates.
5. **Tracking behavior**
   - Opening statement via `View` marks viewed.
   - Clicking `Download` marks downloaded.
6. **Permission parity**
   - Validate role access for Statements now mirrors Invoice/Credit Note style:
     - view/download broad portal roles
     - edit manager+
     - delete admin+
     - import manager+.

```
diff --git a/backend/routes/statements.js b/backend/routes/statements.js
index ce682ba..2bb7601 100644
--- a/backend/routes/statements.js
+++ b/backend/routes/statements.js
@@ -93,8 +93,8 @@ async function markStatementAccess(statement, kind, req) {
   return statement;
 }
 
-// Apply auth and document access check to all routes
-// Statements are GA only for now
+// Apply auth and document access check to all routes.
+// Role access is controlled via STATEMENTS_* permissions.
 router.use(auth);
 router.use(requirePermission('STATEMENTS_VIEW'));
 router.use(checkDocumentAccess);
@@ -1014,6 +1014,68 @@ router.get('/:id/view-pdf', async (req, res) =&gt; {
   }
 });
 
+/**
+ * Stream XLS/XLSX inline for in-browser preview and mark as "viewed"
+ * (not downloaded), mirroring PDF preview semantics.
+ */
+router.get('/:id/view-xls', async (req, res) =&gt; {
+  try {
+    const statement = await Statement.findByPk(req.params.id, {
+      include: [{ model: Company, as: 'company', attributes: ['id', 'name'] }]
+    });
+
+    if (!statement) return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Statement not found' });
+    if (req.accessibleCompanyIds !== null &amp;&amp; !req.accessibleCompanyIds.includes(statement.companyId)) {
+      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Access denied. You do not have access to this statement.' });
+    }
+
+    let stored = statement.xlsFileUrl;
+    if (!stored &amp;&amp; statement.fileUrl &amp;&amp;
+        (statement.fileUrl.toLowerCase().endsWith('.xls') || statement.fileUrl.toLowerCase().endsWith('.xlsx'))) {
+      stored = statement.fileUrl;
+    }
+    if (!stored) {
+      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'No XLS file available for this statement' });
+    }
+
+    const filePath = resolveStatementFile(stored);
+    if (!filePath) {
+      console.error(&#96;Statement XLS not found on disk: ${stored}&#96;);
+      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'XLS file not found on server' });
+    }
+
+    await markStatementAccess(statement, 'view', req);
+
+    await logActivity({
+      type: ActivityType.STATEMENT_VIEWED,
+      userId: req.user.userId,
+      userEmail: req.user.email,
+      userRole: req.user.role,
+      action: &#96;Viewed statement XLS ${statement.statementNumber || statement.id}&#96;,
+      details: {
+        statementId: statement.id,
+        statementNumber: statement.statementNumber,
+        viewMethod: 'xls_preview'
+      },
+      companyId: statement.companyId,
+      companyName: statement.company?.name || null,
+      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
+      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
+    });
+
+    const ext = path.extname(filePath).toLowerCase();
+    const contentType = ext === '.xlsx'
+      ? 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'
+      : 'application/vnd.ms-excel';
+    res.setHeader('Content-Type', contentType);
+    res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', &#96;inline; filename="${path.basename(filePath)}"&#96;);
+    res.sendFile(filePath);
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error('Error viewing statement XLS:', error);
+    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
+  }
+});
+
 /**
  * Generic single-statement download. Accepts ?format=pdf or ?format=xls; defaults to pdf.
  * Records a download access event.
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: git add backend/routes/statements.js backend/utils/permissions.js frontend/src/App.js frontend/src/context/PermissionContext.js frontend/src/pages/Statements.js frontend/src/pages/StatementView.js frontend/src/pages/StatementEdit.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Statements: align actions/permissions with invoice-credit note flows

Move statements to route-based View/Edit pages, add XLS inline view tracking, align STATEMENTS_* role permissions with invoice/credit-note policy, and simplify the table/actions to View/Download/Edit/Delete (including removal of the non-existent Statement No. column).
EOF
)" && git push</summary>

```bash
git add backend/routes/statements.js backend/utils/permissions.js frontend/src/App.js frontend/src/context/PermissionContext.js frontend/src/pages/Statements.js frontend/src/pages/StatementView.js frontend/src/pages/StatementEdit.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Statements: align actions/permissions with invoice-credit note flows

Move statements to route-based View/Edit pages, add XLS inline view tracking, align STATEMENTS_* role permissions with invoice/credit-note policy, and simplify the table/actions to View/Download/Edit/Delete (including removal of the non-existent Statement No. column).
EOF
)" && git push
```

_**Agent (premium)**_

Yes — fixed a few clean ones right away from your list.

### Files changed
- `frontend/src/pages/Statements.js`  
  Replaced pagination `<a href="#">` controls with `<button type="button">` controls to remove `jsx-a11y/anchor-is-valid` warnings on the Statements page.
- `frontend/src/pages/StatementView.js`  
  Fixed the React hook ref cleanup warning by capturing the canvas container ref in a local variable inside the effect (`react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` cleanup warning).
- `frontend/src/components/InactivityLogout.js`  
  Removed unused default `React` import (`no-unused-vars`).

### Commands to run
- `cd frontend && npm run build`

### What to test manually
1. Open `Statements` and use pagination (`« ‹ › »`) — ensure page navigation still works as before.
2. Open a statement `View` page and switch preview types / navigate away — ensure PDF preview still renders and no regression in cleanup behavior.

Exactly — I’ve updated `StatementView` to follow the same UI structure/pattern as Invoice/Credit Note view pages.

### What I changed

- `frontend/src/pages/StatementView.js`
  - Matched page header layout:
    - Title + subtitle on left
    - Right-side buttons now aligned to the same pattern:
      - `Back to Statements`
      - `Download Statement`
  - Removed the extra `Edit` button from the view header (so buttons follow the same style/pattern you asked for).
  - Kept left preview card pattern consistent (PDF/XLS preview card with same visual structure).
  - Reworked right panel to use the same **Information card + list-group rows** style as Invoice/Credit Note pages.
  - Added retention badge in card header via `DocumentRetentionTimer` (same style pattern).
  - Added both:
    - `Workflow Status` (statement lifecycle: draft/sent/etc)
    - `Document Status` (Ready/New, Viewed, Downloaded, etc), same badge semantics as invoices/credit notes.

### Result
The statement view page now looks/behaves like the Invoice/Credit Note view layout instead of a custom simpler page.

### Commands to run
- `cd frontend && npm run build`

### What to test manually
1. Open `Statements` and click `View`.
2. Confirm header buttons are only `Back to Statements` and `Download Statement`.
3. Confirm right-side card matches invoice/credit-note style (list-group rows, retention badge, status badges).
4. Confirm PDF/XLS preview still works and XLS sheet switching still works.

Done — `StatementEdit` now follows the same edit-page pattern as Invoice/Credit Note edit pages.

### What I changed

- `frontend/src/pages/StatementEdit.js`
  - Reworked to the same **2-column layout**:
    - **Left:** preview card (`PDF Preview` / `XLS Preview`)
    - **Right:** editable details card
  - Header now matches the same pattern:
    - page title (`Edit Statement`)
    - single `Back to View` button in header
  - Form actions now sit in the details card footer area:
    - `Save Changes`
    - `Cancel`
  - Added preview support inside edit page:
    - PDF rendering via `pdfjs-dist`
    - XLS rendering via SheetJS with sheet tabs
  - Kept statement-specific fields (period/balances/debits/credits/status/notes/edit reason), but presented in the same style as other edit pages.

### Commands to run
- `cd frontend && npm run build`

### What to test manually
1. Open `Statements` → `Edit` on a statement.
2. Confirm page structure matches Credit Note/Invoice edit (left preview, right details form).
3. Confirm header has `Back to View`.
4. Confirm `Save Changes` and `Cancel` buttons appear in the details panel.
5. Test both PDF-backed and XLS-backed statements preview correctly.

_**Agent (premium)**_

Done — removed that decorative first XLS row from statement previews.

### What changed
- `frontend/src/pages/StatementView.js`
- `frontend/src/pages/StatementEdit.js`

Both now:
- inspect the first worksheet row before rendering HTML
- if row 1 is exactly `STATEMENT` in the first cell and all other cells are empty, preview starts from row 2 (`range = 1`)

So this row is no longer shown:

`STATEMENT | [empty] [empty] [empty] ...`

If you want, I can push this latest tweak now.

_**Agent (premium)**_

You’re right — Statements should use the same document-status model as Invoices/Credit Notes (`Ready (New)`, `Viewed`, `Downloaded`, etc.), and `sent` shouldn’t be the visible status.

I started applying this, but your environment has switched to **read-only sandbox** right now, so I can’t modify files at the moment.

Once write access is back, I’ll make these exact changes:

- `Statements` list:
  - Status filter options: `All`, `Ready (New)`, `Viewed`, `Downloaded`, `Review`, `Queried`
  - Status badge column based on `documentStatus` + `viewedAt/downloadedAt` fallback (same as Invoice/Credit Note)
- `StatementEdit`:
  - Replace old statement lifecycle select (`draft/sent/...`) with `Document Status` select (`ready/review/viewed/downloaded/queried`)
- `StatementView`:
  - Remove/avoid old workflow status wording and show only document-status semantics
- Backend `GET /api/statements`:
  - Interpret status filter like invoice/credit note routes (`ready_new`, `viewed`, `downloaded`, etc.)
- Backend `PUT /api/statements/:id`:
  - Accept and persist `documentStatus` edits (with edit history logging)

If you re-enable write mode, I’ll apply and push this in one pass.

---

_**User**_

We need to also check Permissions.

So they should ONLY be visible by the users that are assigned to the company right? Thats how Invoices and Credit notes work?

e.g. I am a User, I have been assigned to Company of 14. That means when I log in I can see statements, and if my user has the permissions - I get email notifications etc.

We need to be 100000% positive permisions, roles are the same as Invoices/Credit Notes here please? In terms of if I am a Manager, what can I do - can i edit, only view etc. This needs to be the same for Statements.

We also need to make sure that the import routine allows for Statements. In terms of "Import Data"

So for Users - they have these options right?

id - Database ID (UUID) - Optional, used for updating existing users
name - User name (required)
email - Email address (required)
role - User role (external_user, notification_contact, credit_controller, etc.)
active - TRUE or FALSE
all_companies - TRUE or FALSE
company_account_numbers - Comma-separated account numbers
send_invoice_email - TRUE or FALSE
send_invoice_attachment - TRUE or FALSE
send_statement_email - TRUE or FALSE
send_statement_attachment - TRUE or FALSE
send_email_as_summary - TRUE or FALSE
send_import_summary_report - TRUE or FALSE

We need to make sure that we have the logic for if the user needs attachments for PDF/Excel or both?

Can we also add the option to "Download Example File" for both those imports? So we cna just download a template for Companies and Users

---

_**Agent (premium)**_

I can’t apply changes in Ask mode, but I can verify the current logic and give you a precise checklist of what is already correct vs what still needs changing.

I’ll quickly inspect permissions, company-scoping, import fields, and statement attachment behavior across backend/frontend.

_**Agent (premium)**_

_**Agent (premium)**_

Yes — and good call to verify this now. I checked the codebase and here is the exact state.

## Permissions / Company Access Check

### What is correct now
- **Company-scoped visibility is enforced for Statements**: users only see statements for companies they can access (assigned companies + hierarchy rules), same pattern as invoices/credit notes via `checkDocumentAccess` + `buildCompanyFilter`.
- **Per-company access is also re-checked on single-record routes** (`/api/statements/:id`, download/view routes), so users cannot fetch random statement IDs outside their scope.
- **Role matrix definitions are aligned** with invoices/credit notes in both backend and frontend permission maps:
  - `VIEW` / `DOWNLOAD`: `global_admin`, `administrator`, `manager`, `credit_senior`, `credit_controller`, `external_user`
  - `IMPORT` / `EDIT`: `global_admin`, `administrator`, `manager`
  - `DELETE`: `global_admin`, `administrator`

Evidence:
```99:101:backend/routes/statements.js
router.use(auth);
router.use(requirePermission('STATEMENTS_VIEW'));
router.use(checkDocumentAccess);
```

```64:68:backend/utils/permissions.js
STATEMENTS_VIEW: PORTAL_ROLES,
STATEMENTS_IMPORT: ['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager'],
STATEMENTS_EDIT: ['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager'],
STATEMENTS_DELETE: ['global_admin', 'administrator'],
STATEMENTS_DOWNLOAD: PORTAL_ROLES,
```

### Critical mismatch to fix
- `POST /api/statements` and `PUT /api/statements/:id` **do not currently enforce** `requirePermission('STATEMENTS_EDIT')` at route level.
- They only block `external_user`, which means some non-edit roles could still edit/create via API calls.

Evidence:
```688:689:backend/routes/statements.js
router.post('/', async (req, res) => {
```

```786:787:backend/routes/statements.js
router.put('/:id', async (req, res) => {
```

For true parity with invoices/credit notes, those two routes should match this pattern:
```940:940:backend/routes/invoices.js
router.put('/:id', requirePermission('INVOICES_EDIT'), async (req, res) => {
```

---

## Statement Notifications (PDF/XLS split)

### What is correct now
- User/company models have split toggles:
  - `sendStatementPdfAttachment`
  - `sendStatementXlsAttachment`
- Notification service uses those split toggles and can attach PDF, XLS, both, or neither.
- If statement email is off, attachments are effectively ignored.

Evidence:
```167:170:backend/services/documentNotificationService.js
recipient.sendPdfAttachment = !!user.sendStatementPdfAttachment;
recipient.sendXlsAttachment = !!user.sendStatementXlsAttachment;
recipient.sendAttachment = recipient.sendPdfAttachment || recipient.sendXlsAttachment;
```

---

## “Import Data” + User Import Fields

### Current behavior
- **Statements import exists** as a dedicated document flow: `/api/statements/import` (used by `Statements` page).
- **`Import Data` page is currently only for Companies + Users** (not document files), which is expected from current design.

### User import fields
- Backend user import supports **both new split columns and legacy single column**:
  - `send_statement_pdf_attachment`
  - `send_statement_xls_attachment`
  - legacy `send_statement_attachment` maps to both if provided.

Evidence:
```1862:1870:backend/routes/users.js
const sendStatementPdfAttachment = parseBooleanValue(
  row['send_statement_pdf_attachment'] ||
  row['sendStatementPdfAttachment'] ||
  (legacyStatementAttachment !== null ? (legacyStatementAttachment ? 'TRUE' : 'FALSE') : 'FALSE')
);
const sendStatementXlsAttachment = parseBooleanValue(
  row['send_statement_xls_attachment'] ||
  row['sendStatementXlsAttachment'] ||
  (legacyStatementAttachment !== null ? (legacyStatementAttachment ? 'TRUE' : 'FALSE') : 'FALSE')
);
```

### Mismatches in UI/docs
- `ImportData` page still documents old `send_statement_attachment` text (outdated).
- Users export CSV column list still references old single field in one place, despite export data containing split fields.

---

## “Download Example File” for Companies and Users

### Current state
- No “Download Example File” button in `ImportData` UI right now.
- Companies route does not expose a dedicated import template endpoint.
- There is a template endpoint in `backend/routes/userImport.js`, but your active `/api/users` import flow is in `backend/routes/users.js`, so this is not the reliable path for your current UI flow.

---

If you switch back to Agent mode, I can implement this in one pass:
1. enforce `STATEMENTS_EDIT` on statement create/update routes,
2. update Import Data user-field docs to split statement attachments,
3. add explicit **Download Example File** buttons for both Companies and Users using stable endpoints.

---

_**Agent (premium)**_

Permissions for Statements are now aligned to the same logic pattern as Invoices/Credit Notes (view routes open to authenticated document-access scope, edit/create/import/delete controlled by permission middleware).

### What I changed

- **Backend parity in `statements` routes**
  - Removed global `STATEMENTS_VIEW` gate from `router.use(...)` so base access flow matches invoices/credit notes (`auth` + `checkDocumentAccess`).
  - Added explicit `requirePermission('STATEMENTS_EDIT')` on:
    - `POST /api/statements`
    - `PUT /api/statements/:id`
- **Frontend route parity**
  - `statements` list and `statements/:id/view` now follow same pattern as invoices/credit notes (no `PermissionRoute` wrapper).
  - `statements/:id/edit` remains permission-gated (`STATEMENTS_EDIT`) like invoice/credit-note edit pages.
- **Sidebar parity**
  - Statements nav item now follows invoice/credit-note pattern (always shown like those menus, not wrapped in `STATEMENTS_VIEW` check).

---

### Role permissions (now aligned)

From the shared permission map used by backend + frontend:

- **View / Download Statements**: `global_admin`, `administrator`, `manager`, `credit_senior`, `credit_controller`, `external_user`
- **Import Statements**: `global_admin`, `administrator`, `manager`
- **Edit/Create Statements**: `global_admin`, `administrator`, `manager`
- **Delete Statements**: `global_admin`, `administrator`

Company scoping is still enforced via `checkDocumentAccess` + `buildCompanyFilter`, so users only see statements for assigned/accessed companies (or all if `allCompanies=true` / admin scope).

---

**Files changed**
- `backend/routes/statements.js` — removed global view middleware and added route-level `STATEMENTS_EDIT` enforcement on create/update.
- `frontend/src/App.js` — made Statements list/view route access pattern match invoices/credit-notes; kept edit gated.
- `frontend/src/components/Layout/Layout.js` — made Statements menu visibility pattern match invoice/credit-note nav behavior.

**Commands to run**
- `cd frontend && npm run build`

**What to test manually**
- Log in as **manager** assigned to company `14`: confirm can view list/view page/edit page for statements in that company.
- Log in as **credit_controller** assigned to company `14`: confirm can view/download statements but **cannot** access edit page.
- Log in as **external_user** assigned to company `14`: confirm can view/download only assigned-company statements.
- Try creating/updating statement via API as non-edit role: should return `403`.
- Try opening statement from an unassigned company: should return access denied / not visible in list.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: git diff -- frontend/src/App.js frontend/src/components/Layout/Layout.js backend/routes/statements.js</summary>

```bash
git diff -- frontend/src/App.js frontend/src/components/Layout/Layout.js backend/routes/statements.js
```

```
diff --git a/backend/routes/statements.js b/backend/routes/statements.js
index 2bb7601..67b304a 100644
--- a/backend/routes/statements.js
+++ b/backend/routes/statements.js
@@ -93,10 +93,9 @@ async function markStatementAccess(statement, kind, req) {
   return statement;
 }
 
-// Apply auth and document access check to all routes.
-// Role access is controlled via STATEMENTS_* permissions.
+// Apply auth and company-scoped document access checks to all routes.
+// Route-level permissions for create/edit/delete/import mirror invoices/credit notes.
 router.use(auth);
-router.use(requirePermission('STATEMENTS_VIEW'));
 router.use(checkDocumentAccess);
 
 // Get all statements (filtered by user's accessible companies)
@@ -144,8 +143,38 @@ router.get('/', async (req, res) =&gt; {
       whereConditions.companyId = { [Op.in]: Array.from(expandedIds) };
     }
     
-    if (status) {
-      whereConditions.status = status;
+    // Document status filter (ready_new, viewed, downloaded, queried, review) - same as invoices/credit notes
+    if (status &amp;&amp; status !== 'all') {
+      const statusConditions = [];
+      if (status === 'ready_new') {
+        statusConditions.push({
+          documentStatus: 'ready',
+          viewedAt: null
+        });
+      } else if (status === 'viewed') {
+        statusConditions.push({
+          [Op.or]: [
+            { documentStatus: 'viewed' },
+            Sequelize.literal(&#96;("Statement"."viewedAt" IS NOT NULL AND "Statement"."downloadedAt" IS NULL)&#96;)
+          ]
+        });
+      } else if (status === 'downloaded') {
+        statusConditions.push({
+          [Op.or]: [
+            { documentStatus: 'downloaded' },
+            Sequelize.literal(&#96;"Statement"."downloadedAt" IS NOT NULL&#96;)
+          ]
+        });
+      } else if (status === 'queried') {
+        statusConditions.push({ documentStatus: 'queried' });
+      } else if (status === 'review') {
+        statusConditions.push({ documentStatus: 'review' });
+      }
```
